subject: [BUG] fuse: wb_wait_for_completion hang on suspend with fuse-overlayfs on tmpfs persists in 6.19.6 (AS_NO_DATA_INTEGRITY fix incomplete)

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author confirms that the issue is resolved and agrees to submit a revised patch for review
reviews.raw_body:
Great, I will submit this patch to the mailing list today. Thank you
for verifying!

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that the issue is not fully resolved and needs further investigation, as their repro triggers the hang even before tmp pages are removed.
reviews.raw_body:
(fwiw, my repro triggers the hang even prior to the tmp pages being
removed, so I think the firefox + youtube scenario you're running into
is indeed the folio_prepare_writeback() scenario)

2026-03-18.patch_summary: This patch fixes a hang issue in the Linux kernel's fuse module, specifically when using fuse-overlayfs on tmpfs and suspending the system. The problem occurs because the wb_wait_for_completion function is waiting for writeback to complete on an inode that has already been marked as dirty and under writeback. To fix this, the patch adds a check to skip inodes with no data integrity guarantees (e.g., FUSE mappings) when syncing. This approach ensures that the system does not hang indefinitely while waiting for writeback to complete on such inodes.
